title: serratula flavescens subsp. leucantha
intro: Serratula flavescens subsp. leucantha, also known as the yellow milkwort, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae that is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, the plant's medicinal properties have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its potential health benefits.
